compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
R4 GROUNDING + BLOCKER (2026-07-18, investigation before rewrite/claim). Drove the "rewrite plan -> pass gate -> implement -> land" pipeline; STOPPED at a hard blocker and two grounding corrections. No ticket/description mutation, no claim, no code landed.

BLOCKER (fatal to implement/land): R4's retrospective gate's primary anchor -- ">=8/10 of E2's class-4 (unnecessary-mechanism) mutants, <=1/10 controls" -- is UNRUNNABLE because E2's mutant corpus DOES NOT EXIST. There is no docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e2_mutant_corpus.jsonl (verified: find, `git ls-files`, `git log --all`). E2 (d125, crude-volumed-scaup) is in_progress, not closed; its own description says the corpus + assemble_e2_mutants.py harness + baseline table are what it WILL produce. Epic 6982's 2026-07-16 comment confirms "the E-experiments (E2/E3/E5/E6) + R-implementations (R1-R7) are unrun/unimplemented." `rebar ready` does NOT list R4 -- rebar treats it as blocked on depends_on d125. Per the epic rule "no detection-bearing recommendation implemented without its eval gate," R4 must not land until E2 delivers class-4 mutants.

GROUNDING CORRECTION 1 -- 5886 does NOT cleanly serve the NECESSITY probe (it serves the BUG TIER). 5886-d028-7954-4763 (unblacked-clownish-dunlin, bug) is a legit fix: unmapped inbound Jira status defaulted to "open", silently reopening closed tickets. Its plan HAS explicit Reproduction Steps + Expected/Actual, so it demonstrates necessity well -> the necessity probe would correctly NOT fire on it. The stub's "its plan asserted a fix for behavior that already worked" is unsupported: behavior was genuinely broken, and its reuse claim ("fetcher.py already emits fetcher-unmapped-jira-status") was TRUE -- that alert landed 2026-07-06 (git log -S, commit 9d5c9ba2d), 2 days before 5886's claim (2026-07-08). Epic E1 classifies the 3 misses (dc58/db7b/5886) as "plan asserts a capability an existing module lacks" = absent-capability (R1/class-1), NOT over-action (R4/class-4). => R4 needs a VALID class-4 positive fixture; 5886 is not one.

GROUNDING CORRECTION 2 (confirms bug-tier premise) -- 5886's persisted plan review was an EXEMPT PASS: REVIEW_RESULT (git 44c01137c) = {"runner":"exempt","verdict":"PASS","findings":[],"llm_calls":0,"coverage":{"exempt":"bug tickets are exempt from the plan-review gate"}}. So "today bugs skip substantive plan review" is CONFIRMED. 5886 IS a good fixture for piece (b) the bug tier (a real bug that got only an exempt PASS), just not for piece (a) the necessity probe.

SEAM GROUNDING (for when unblocked). Bug-tier claim-gate seam: workflow_ops.plan_review_precheck (workflow_ops.py:135-160) short-circuits ticket_type in ("bug","session_log","code_review","identity") to orchestrator._exempt_verdict (orchestrator.py:676) -- no DET floor, no LLM. R4's bug tier runs DET floor + necessity probe here (advisory). Necessity probe: a new advisory criterion added to criteria_routing.json (entry shape: exec/facet/applies_at{scope,suppress_types,suppress_when}/block_threshold/default_posture/checklist[{key,check}]) with default_posture:"advisory" and, for the bug tier, NOT suppressing "bug" in suppress_types (contrast A1/E4/G7 which all suppress_types:["bug"]); the id MUST also be registered in registry CANONICAL_LLM or registry.validate_packaged_routing() (registry.py:445) flags it ORPHAN and fails CI (per R2's grounding-correction comment on this epic). Pass-1 fan-out is pass1.run_pass1.

RECOMMENDATION: (1) complete E2 (d125) to commit the class-4 mutant corpus + baseline; (2) select/construct a valid class-4 positive necessity fixture (5886 is not one -- reassign 5886 to the bug-tier fixture role); then (3) resume R4: claim -> implement advisory necessity criterion + bug tier -> validate >=8/10 class-4 discrimination on E2's corpus + bug-tier lifecycle test on 5886 -> land via Gerrit -> close. Alternatively, if R4 should not wait on E2, an operator decision is needed on whether R4 may generate its own deterministic class-4 fixtures (forking E2's centralized corpus) -- not taken unilaterally here.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

FixedBench's 35-65% over-action result has no plan-review counterpart: no criterion asks
whether a proposed change is actually *needed*. Separately, bug tickets are gate-exempt — the
plan-review gate short-circuits every `bug` to a bare exempt-PASS
(`workflow_ops.plan_review_precheck` src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:150-160 →
`orchestrator._exempt_verdict` src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:676), so a bug gets no
substantive review at all (verified on bug 5886: its persisted REVIEW_RESULT was
`{"runner":"exempt","verdict":"PASS","llm_calls":0}`).

This story ships two coupled ADVISORY pieces. Prior art: T1's FixedBench over-action finding
(the over-action gap this probe closes) and the R1/R3 advisory-criterion pattern
(`asserted-capability`, `decomp-shape`) — registered advisory, calibrated by
`rebar criteria eval`, auto-monitored by the standing recorder (d8a5).

## Advisory-first strategy (re-plan — supersedes the stub)

The stub gated R4 on "retrospective eval on E2's class-4 (unnecessary-mechanism) mutant
corpus". That corpus does NOT exist on main and E2/E3 batch evals are PERMANENTLY REJECTED on
cost, so this re-plan DROPS the E2 dependency (`depends_on d125` removed) and validates with
BOUNDED, HAND-AUTHORED fixtures run through `rebar criteria eval` (<=~8 live single-criterion
runs, NOT a batch). Both pieces ship ADVISORY (never block). Post-ship effectiveness is dogfood
monitoring via the standing recorder + the advisory->blocking promotion gate — recorded as an
`[operator-attested]` observation, not a config flag.

## What — piece (a): necessity / no-op probe (pass-1 advisory)

A new pass-1 advisory criterion `necessity` that flags a plan which does NOT demonstrate the
change is needed — current behavior neither reproduced nor motivated (the FixedBench over-action
gap: a plan that adds a mechanism without establishing the need). It ACCEPTS justified no-op /
docs-test-only / well-motivated outcomes (a plan with reproduction + motivation PASSES). It is a
single-turn (1-TURN) plan-text judgment, DISTINCT from R1's `asserted-capability` (which greps
for an already-existing implementation): `necessity` asks whether the plan MOTIVATES the change
at all, not whether a named module already provides it.

- `criteria_routing.json`: new `necessity` entry — `exec:"1-TURN"`, `facet:"scope-intent"`,
  `default_posture:"advisory"`, `block_threshold:0.95`, `applies_at.scope:["leaf"]`, and
  deliberately NO `suppress_types` (so it applies to `bug` — required by piece (b); contrast
  F1/F4/A1/G7 which all `suppress_types:["bug"]`).
- `registry.py`: register `necessity` in `CANONICAL_LLM` (so
  `registry.validate_packaged_routing()` does NOT flag it ORPHAN and fail CI).
- `reviewers/plan_review_necessity.md`: the rubric prompt (gate sub-answer + grounded
  sub-answer), and regenerate `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md`.
- Wire it into the production Pass-1 finder batch (`workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` `criteria:`
  list, gated by `when: include_necessity`) so it actually runs on leaf plan reviews.

## What — piece (b): lightweight bug review tier

Bugs stop short-circuiting to bare exempt-PASS; instead the plan-review gate runs a LIGHT
ADVISORY tier = the DET floor + the `necessity` probe, never blocking a bug.

- `workflow_ops.plan_review_precheck`: for `ticket_type == "bug"`, run the DET floor, DOWNGRADE
  all DET findings to advisory (`det_blocking:[]` — a bug is never blocked), set `run_llm:True`,
  and stamp `coverage.bug_tier`. (`session_log`/`code_review`/`identity` stay exempt.)
- `workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria`: for `ticket_type == "bug"`, restrict the
  included LLM criteria to `registry.BUG_TIER_CRITERIA` (= `("necessity",)`) so a bug gets the
  DET floor + necessity only (the light tier), not the full suite.
- `registry.py`: add `BUG_TIER_CRITERIA = ("necessity",)`.
- The CLI claim-time bug exemption (`_commands/gates.py:93`) is UNCHANGED — a bug still needs no
  signed attestation to be claimed; this tier only makes an explicit `rebar review-plan <bug>`
  (and the gate run) produce a substantive advisory review instead of exempt-PASS.

## Approach rationale

Reuse the R1/R3 advisory-criterion seam wholesale (routing entry + `CANONICAL_LLM` + rubric +
`rebar criteria eval` fixture + auto-monitoring) rather than a bespoke path — it is the proven,
lint-clean, non-orphan pattern. The bug tier reuses the existing `probe_criteria`-style
criteria restriction (route_criteria/assemble) rather than a new engine branch: bugs flow the
SAME find->verify->decide passes, just with a one-criterion included set and DET downgraded to
advisory, so the "never block a bug" invariant is structural (advisory posture + empty
det_blocking), not a special-case in the decision kernel.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/`: `criteria_routing.json` (necessity entry), `registry.py`
  (CANONICAL_LLM + BUG_TIER_CRITERIA), `workflow_ops.py` (bug-tier precheck + assemble),
  `reviewers/plan_review_necessity.md`.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/plan-review.yaml` (finder batch slot).
- `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-necessity.eval.yaml` (bounded sanity fixtures).
- `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` (regenerated), `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (bug tier +
  necessity probe + promotion gate).
- `tests/unit/` deterministic unit tests; `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/` sanity
  artifacts.
- Module-size: hard 800-LOC absolute cap on any `src/rebar` file (no allowlist); if a touched
  file would exceed 800, extract a new module. Sequenced AFTER the floor-split (dep 7d91).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both pieces are registered ADVISORY and non-orphan:
  `python -c "import json,rebar.llm.plan_review.registry as r; d=json.load(open('src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json')); assert d['necessity']['default_posture']=='advisory'; assert 'bug' not in d['necessity'].get('applies_at',{}).get('suppress_types',[]); assert 'necessity' in r.CANONICAL_LLM; assert r.validate_packaged_routing()==[]; assert r.BUG_TIER_CRITERIA==('necessity',)"`
  exits 0.
- [ ] Deterministic (no-LLM) unit tests pin the non-LLM logic and pass:
  `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_necessity_criterion.py tests/unit/test_bug_review_tier.py -q`
  green — registration/routing/prompt-contract/fixture-shape/guide-section/effectiveness
  auto-inclusion for necessity, AND the bug-tier lifecycle (precheck emits `run_llm=True` +
  `det_blocking==[]` + `coverage.bug_tier` for a bug; assemble restricts a bug's included set to
  `necessity`; the tier never yields a blocking verdict for a bug).
- [ ] Bounded live sanity committed (<=~8 live single-criterion runs, NOT an E2/E3 batch): the
  necessity probe FIRES on an over-action positive (adds a mechanism it does not motivate) and is
  CLEAN on a well-motivated negative — `rebar criteria eval necessity --output json` recall 1.0
  over the positives / false-accept 0.0 over the negatives, committed to
  `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/necessity_sanity.json`. AND the bug tier gives bug 5886
  a light advisory review instead of a bare exempt-PASS (verdict `runner != "exempt"`, the
  necessity probe ran, no blocking), committed to
  `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/bug_tier_sanity.json`.
  (5886 is NOT a necessity-probe fixture — it is a well-motivated absent-capability bug, so it is
  the bug-tier lifecycle fixture, not an over-action positive.)
- [ ] Both criteria auto-flow into the standing recorder with zero wiring:
  `python docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/criterion_effectiveness.py --report`
  includes `necessity` when a REVIEW_RESULT cites it (pinned by the effectiveness unit test).
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` documents the bug review tier and the necessity probe, and the
  advisory->blocking promotion gate for both (citing the standing per-criterion effectiveness
  recorder protocol, epic d8a5).
- [ ] [operator-attested] The advisory->blocking promotion decision is dogfood-gated on the
  standing recorder's trailing metrics (high detection_proxy, low blocking_fp_proxy, sufficient
  sample) — recorded as an observation expectation on this ticket, met by the Gerrit change
  landing MERGED with both gate votes green (change id / Verified+1 / LLM-Review+1 recorded on
  the ticket), not by an in-session command.
compiled_state.title: R4: necessity/no-op probe + lightweight bug review tier (gated retrospective)
